Read this plot summary, from the cartoon: ''Kang Vs the Avengers, Avengers: Earth's Mightiest Heroes (2010-2013), (clips on youtube): '' ''Ravonna,  was caught in temporal anomalies and if not for Kang's technology would have been purged from existence. She remains in stasis, while Kang travels back to the 21st century to kill Captain America, whose creation he identifies as the source of the problem, and prepare Earth's armies for the Skrull-Kree war, all to save his one true love''.  Isnt THAT something we would watch? compared to what we get:   ''Ant man gets sucked into quantum realm which for some reason has humanoids living there (did they evolve from apes like we did?), which includes Bill Murray dressed like a 17th century Musketeer, to face Kang who...cant really do anything. Then ants come and beat him.''

Visuals are great for the most part, except for some scenes in the first half.

Cassie Lang was a lot more likeable than the other young heroes we’ve been shown so far and a worthy addition.

For a movie containing multiverse and quantum realm material, it was surprisingly easy to follow and a lot less messy than Doctor Strange 2.

Both post credit scenes are enormously important, easily compared to Iron Man post credit scene and Avengers 1 post credit scene.

Great way to kick off phase 5.
